pricing.source: OpenAI API pricing, standard short-context rates, 2026-06-24

hardChecks.label: Citation source ids
hardChecks.expectation: Every cited source id exists in the source packet

hardChecks.label: Cost metadata
hardChecks.expectation: estimatedUsd is present when pricing is configured

hardChecks.label: Token metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Input, cached-input, and output token counts are coherent

hardChecks.label: Provider and model metadata
hardChecks.expectation: Provider, model, and prompt version are present

hardChecks.label: Judge input boundary
hardChecks.expectation: Judge prompt omits eval-only object keys and holdout tuning fields

hardChecks.label: Judge claim coverage
hardChecks.expectation: LLM judge returns exactly one judgment for every briefing claim

claimJudgments.claimText: Candidate prompt increased median token usage by 11% while improving latency by 6% because fewer outputs needed manual reruns (not because single generations were shorter).
claimJudgments.explanation: S1 directly states token usage rose 11%, latency improved 6%, and the latency gain came from fewer repeated/manual reruns rather than shorter single generations.

claimJudgments.claimText: Finance will approve demo-quality runs up to the reference target cost budget if the report shows why extra tokens reduce reviewer time; exceeding the budget requires smaller batch size, stricter retry caps, or more aggressive trace summarization.
claimJudgments.explanation: S2 states finance approved demo-quality eval runs up to the reference target cost budget if extra tokens are justified by reduced review time, and says exceeding the budget requires reducing batch size, capping retries, or summarizing traces more aggressively.

claimJudgments.claimText: Reviewers find long traces useful only when summarized next to the failing claim; raw provider payload dumps slow review because evidence is hard to find.
claimJudgments.explanation: S3 says long traces are useful only when summarized next to the failed claim, while raw provider payloads slowed review because reviewers had to search for relevant evidence.

claimJudgments.claimText: Live demo felt responsive when a single briefing finished under ~10 seconds; stakeholders tolerated longer full runs if progress/artifact paths updated continuously.
claimJudgments.explanation: S4 supports both the under-10-second single-briefing responsiveness threshold and tolerance for longer full eval runs when progress and artifact paths update continuously.

claimJudgments.claimText: Draft retry policy proposes one automatic retry for transient provider errors, requires a manifest entry when retries change cost/latency, and rejects silent repeated generations.
claimJudgments.explanation: S5 directly states the policy allows one automatic retry for transient provider errors, requires a manifest entry when retries change cost or latency, and rejects silent repeated generations.

missingImportantEvidence: S6 is important support for the recommendation to keep detailed traces on disk while showing short dashboard summaries with selected source ids, missing evidence, retry count, and final output path; the briefing uses this content but does not cite S6 in its cited source list.
recommendationJudgment.explanation: The recommendation is well aligned with the packet: keep summarized claim-level traces in the UI, avoid raw payload dumps, retain detailed traces on disk, cap retries at one for transient provider errors, log retry cost/latency effects, and reduce batch size or summarize traces if budget is threatened. It balances reviewer feedback, finance constraints, retry policy, and demo responsiveness without claiming unlimited trace retention or retries. The main weakness is citation coverage: the detailed trace-summary mock and full-traces-on-disk recommendation are supported by S6, which is not included in the citedSourceIds list.

citationSupport.note: S1 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S2 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S3 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S4 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.
S5 appears in fully supported claim-level judgments.

notes: Hybrid evaluator output: deterministic hard checks plus structured LLM judge evidence. Manual spot checks are still required before claiming product improvement.
